blocks:
Sūrat al-Qamar

Makkan, and it consists of fifty-five verses by consensus. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) اقْتَرَبَتِ السَّاعَةُ ** (1) The Hour has drawn near, Al-Qummi said: The Resurrection (القيامة) has drawn near, and there will be nothing after the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله except the Resurrection (القيامة), and the prophethood (النبوّة) and the message (الرسالة) have ended. He said: And it is also narrated that he said: The advent (خروج) of the Qā’im عليه السلام.

** وَانْشَقَّ الْقَمَرُ. and the moon has split. In al-Majma', narrated from Ibn 'Abbās: The polytheists (المشركون) gathered before the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and said: "If you are truthful, split the moon into two halves (فرقتين) for us." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to them: "If I do this, will you believe?" They said: "Yes." It was the night of a full moon (ليلة بدر). So, he asked his Lord to grant Him what they requested, and the moon split into two halves (فرقتين), and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was calling out: "O so-and-so, O so-and-so, bear witness!" And from Jubayr ibn Mut'im: The moon split during the time of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله until it became two halves (فرقتين), one over this mountain and one over that mountain. Some people said: "Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله has bewitched us." A man then said: "If he has bewitched you, he has not bewitched all people."

Al-Qummi narrated it from al-Sādiq عليه السلام in another manner (بنحو آخر). And there is what is in it. He said in al-Majma': The Glorified One only mentioned the drawing near of the Hour (اقتراب الساعة) along with the splitting of the moon (انشقاق القمر) because its splitting is one of the signs (علامة) of the prophethood (نبوّة) of our Prophet, and His prophethood and His time are among the signs (آيات) of the drawing near of the Hour (اقتراب الساعة).

(٢) وَإِنْ يَرَوْا آيَةً يُعْرِضُوا وَيَقُولُوا سِحْرٌ مُسْتَمِرٌّ ** (2) And if they see a sign, they turn away and say, 'A continuous magic!' Continuous (مطّرد). And al-Qummi said: meaning sound (صحيح). And it is said: firm (محكم) from (المرّة). It is said: "I made it firm (أمررته) so it became firm (فاستمرّ)" when you made it firm (أحكمته) so it became firmly established (فاستحكم). (٣) وَكَذَّبُوا وَاتَّبَعُوا أَهْواءَهُمْ ** (3) They denied and followed their desires. And that is what Satan (الشيطان) beautified for them, which is the rejection of the truth after its manifestation. Al-Qummi said: meaning they used to act according to their opinion (رأيهم) and deny their prophets (أنبيائهم). ** وَكُلُّ أَمْرٍ مُسْتَقِرٌّ ** (And every matter is settled) means ending in a goal (غاية). (٤) وَلَقَدْ جاءَهُمْ مِنَ الْأَنْباءِ ما فِيهِ مُزْدَجَرٌ ** (4) Certainly there came to them accounts containing a deterrent. Meaning an admonition (متّعظ) concerning punishment (تعذيب) or threat (وعيد). (٥) حِكْمَةٌ بالِغَةٌ ** (5) A perfect wisdom. Its ultimate goal (غايتها), with no flaw (خلل) in it. فَما تُغْنِ النُّذُرُ ** But the warnings are of no avail. A negation (نفي) or an interrogative of denial (استفهام إنكار). (٦) فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ** (6) So turn away from them. Due to your knowledge that the warning (الإنذار) will not benefit them. يَوْمَ يَدْعُ الدَّاعِ إِلى شَيْءٍ نُكُرٍ ** The day the caller calls to a terrible thing. A dreadful thing (فظيع) that souls deny (تنكره) because they have not known its like (مثله). Al-Qummi said: Imam (peace be upon him) said: "When he comes out, he calls them to what they deny (ينكرون)." And it is said that it is the terror (هول) of the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), and what supports this will follow. And (نُكُرٍ) was read with takhfif (lightening of the vowel/consonant, i.e., نُكْرٍ).

(٧) خُشَّعاً أَبْصارُهُمْ يَخْرُجُونَ مِنَ الْأَجْداثِ ** (With downcast eyes, they will emerge from the graves) meaning, they will emerge from their graves, their eyes humbled and abased due to the terror. كَأَنَّهُمْ جَرادٌ مُنْتَشِرٌ ** (as if they were scattered locusts) in terms of multitude, surging movement, and spreading out in the places. (٨) مُهْطِعِينَ إِلَى الدَّاعِ ** (hastening toward the caller) rushing, stretching out their necks toward Him, or looking toward Him. Al-Qummi (said): When He returns, He will say: "Return." يَقُولُ الْكافِرُونَ هذا يَوْمٌ عَسِرٌ** (The faithless will say, 'This is a hard day!') difficult.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him), from his father, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in a narration concerning the Day of Resurrection, he said: Then the Almighty (الجبّار), blessed and exalted is He, will look down upon them from above His Throne, in shades of angels. Then He will command an angel from the angels to call out among them: "O assembly of creation! Be silent and listen to the caller of the Almighty (الجبّار)." He said: Then the last of them will hear just as the first of them hears. He said: Then their voices will break at that moment, their eyes will be humbled, their flanks (فرائصهم) will tremble, and their hearts will be terrified. And they will raise their heads toward the direction of the sound, hastening (مهطعين) toward the Caller (الدّاع). He said: It is at that moment that the disbeliever will say: هذا يَوْمٌ عَسِرٌ. (This is a hard day!)

(٩) كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ فَكَذَّبُوا عَبْدَنا وَقالُوا مَجْنُونٌ وَازْدُجِرَ ** (9) Before them the people of Noah denied, and they denied Our servant, and said, 'A madman!' and he was reviled. ** كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ ** before your people (قبل قومك). ** قَوْمُ نُوحٍ فَكَذَّبُوا عَبْدَنا ** Noah (نوحاً). ** وَقالُوا مَجْنُونٌ وَازْدُجِرَ ** And he was deterred (وزجر) from conveying the message by various types of harm (بأنواع الاذيّة). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: meaning they harmed him (اذوه) and intended to stone him (وأرادوا رجمه). (١٠) فَدَعا رَبَّهُ أَنِّي مَغْلُوبٌ فَانْتَصِرْ ** (10) So he supplicated his Lord: 'I am overcome, so help [me]!' So take vengeance for me against them (فانتقم لي منهم), and that was after he despaired of them (وذلك بعد يأسه منهم). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: Noah remained among them for one thousand years minus fifty years, calling them secretly and openly. When they refused and became insolent (عتوا), he said: "My Lord, أَنِّي مَغْلُوبٌ فَانْتَصِرْ."

(١١) فَفَتَحْنا أَبْوابَ السَّماءِ بِماءٍ مُنْهَمِرٍ ** (11) So We opened the gates of the sky with pouring water, pouring forth (منصبّ). This is an exaggeration (مبالغة) and a metaphor (تمثيل) for the abundance of the rains and the intensity of their downpour. (١٢) وَفَجَّرْنَا الْأَرْضَ عُيُوناً ** and We made the entire earth as if it were bursting springs (عيون منفجرة). Its origin (أصله) is "and We made the springs (عيون) of the earth burst forth," but it was altered for the sake of exaggeration (للمبالغة). فَالْتَقَى الْماءُ the water of the sky and the water of the earth عَلى أَمْرٍ قَدْ قُدِرَ which Allah, the Mighty and the Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), had decreed. (12) and We made the earth burst forth with springs. So the waters met for a matter that had been decreed. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them both), he said: "Not a single drop of rain descended from the sky except by a counted number (بعدد معدود) and a known weight (ووزن معلوم), except for what occurred on the day of the Flood (الطّوفان) during the time of Noah (peace be upon him), for indeed, pouring water (ماء منهمر) descended without weight or number."

(١٣) وَحَمَلْناهُ عَلى ذاتِ أَلْواحٍ وَدُسُرٍ ** (13) and We carried him on a vessel made of planks and nails, ** وَحَمَلْناهُ عَلى ذاتِ أَلْواحٍ ** (and We carried him on a vessel made of planks) means: possessing wide timbers (أخشاب عريضة). وَدُسُرٍ ** (and nails). Al-Qummi said: The planks (الألواح) are the ship (السّفينة), and the nails (الدّسر) are the pegs (المسامير). He said: And it was said that al-Dusr (الدّسر) is a type of grass (ضرب من الحشيش) with which the ship was fastened. (١٤) تَجْرِي بِأَعْيُنِنا جَزاءً لِمَنْ كانَ كُفِرَ ** (14) sailing under Our observation, a retribution for him who was disbelieved. تَجْرِي بِأَعْيُنِنا ** (sailing under Our observation) means: under Our sight (بمرأىٍ منّا). Al-Qummi said: by Our command (بأمرنا) and Our preservation (وحفظنا). جَزاءً لِمَنْ كانَ كُفِرَ ** (a retribution for him who was disbelieved). Meaning: We did that as a recompense (جزاء) for Noah (peace be upon him), because he was a blessing (نعمة) which they disbelieved in (كفروها), for every prophet is a blessing from Allah and a mercy upon his nation (أمّته). (١٥) وَلَقَدْ تَرَكْناها آيَةً فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ ** (15) Certainly We left it as a sign; so is there anyone who will be admonished? ** وَلَقَدْ تَرَكْناها آيَةً ** (Certainly We left it as a sign) means: it is spoken of (يعبّر بها) since its news became widespread (شاع خبرها). فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ ** (so is there anyone who will be admonished?) means: one who takes heed (معتبر). (١٦) فَكَيْفَ كانَ عَذابِي وَنُذُرِ ** (16) So how was My punishment and My warnings? ** فَكَيْفَ كانَ عَذابِي وَنُذُرِ ** (So how was My punishment and My warnings?) means: My warnings (انذاراتي) or My messengers (رسلي). The entirety of this story has already passed in Surat Hud (سورة هود). (١٧) وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا الْقُرْآنَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ ** (17) Certainly We have made the Quran easy for admonishment; so is there anyone who will be admonished? ** وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا الْقُرْآنَ ** (Certainly We have made the Quran easy) means: We made it easy (سهّلناه). لِلذِّكْرِ ** (for admonishment) means: for remembrance (للاذكار) and taking heed (والاتّعاظ) for whoever remembers (يذّكر), by diversifying within it various types of admonitions (المواعظ) and lessons (والعبر). فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ ** (so is there anyone who will be admonished?) means: one who takes heed (متّعظ). (١٨) كَذَّبَتْ عادٌ فَكَيْفَ كانَ عَذابِي وَنُذُرِ ** (18) 'Ad denied; so how was My punishment and My warnings? كَذَّبَتْ عادٌ فَكَيْفَ كانَ عَذابِي وَنُذُرِ ** ('Ad denied; so how was My punishment and My warnings?) means: and a warning (وإنذار) came to them concerning the punishment before its descent (نزوله). (١٩) إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحاً صَرْصَراً فِي يَوْمِ نَحْسٍ مُسْتَمِرٍّ ** (19) We sent against them a freezing wind on a day of continuous ill-omen, ** إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحاً صَرْصَراً ** (We sent against them a freezing wind) means: cold (باردة). فِي يَوْمِ نَحْسٍ ** (on a day of ill-omen) means: misfortune (شؤم). مُسْتَمِرٍّ ** (continuous) meaning: its ill-omen (شؤمه) continued until the like of it. In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Wednesday (الأربعاء) is a day of continuous ill-omen (نحس مستمرّ) because it is the first day and the last day of the days concerning which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: سَخَّرَها عَلَيْهِمْ سَبْعَ لَيالٍ وَثَمانِيَةَ أَيَّامٍ حُسُوماً. (He subjected it to them for seven nights and eight days continuously.) And in al-Uyun, narrated through Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And in al-Majma', narrated through al-Ayyashi from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): that it was on a Wednesday (يوم الأربعاء). And al-Ayyashi added: on the last day of the month (في آخر الشهر), which does not recur (لا يدور). And in al-Faqih and al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has armies of wind (جنودا من الرّيح) with which He punishes those who disobey Him. An obedient angel (ملك مطاع) is entrusted with every wind among them. When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, intends to punish a people with a punishment, Allah reveals to the angel entrusted with that type of wind which He intends to punish them with. The angel then commands it, and it stirs up (تهيّج) just as the angry lion stirs up. And every wind among them has a name. Do you not hear the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحاً صَرْصَراً فِي يَوْمِ نَحْسٍ مُسْتَمِرٍّ ** (We sent against them a freezing wind on a day of continuous ill-omen)? And in al-Kafi: that which has the same meaning (ما في معناه).

(٢٠) تَنْزِعُ النَّاسَ ** (20) tearing away the people) uprooting them. It was narrated: that they entered the ravines (الشعاب) and the pits (الحفر), and some of them clung to others, but the wind tore them away from them and struck them down dead. ** كَأَنَّهُمْ أَعْجازُ نَخْلٍ مُنْقَعِرٍ ** (as if they were trunks of uprooted palm trees.) The roots of date palms uprooted from their planting places (مغارسه), fallen upon the earth. It was said they were likened to the trunks (الاعجاز) because the wind blew away their heads and cast down their bodies.

(٢١) فَكَيْفَ كانَ عَذابِي وَنُذُرِ ** (21) How was My punishment and My warnings? He repeated it for magnification (of the matter). And it was said that the first refers to what encompassed them in the world, and the second refers to what will encompass them in the Hereafter, just as He also said regarding their story: لِنُذِيقَهُمْ عَذابَ الْخِزْيِ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَلَعَذابُ الْآخِرَةِ أَخْزى ** that We may make them taste the punishment of disgrace in the life of the world, and the punishment of the Hereafter is surely more disgraceful. And the complete story has already passed in Sūrat al-A‘rāf and Hūd.

(٢٢) وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا الْقُرْآنَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ. (22) Certainly We have made the Quran easy for admonishment; so is there anyone who will be admonished? (٢٣) كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ بِالنُّذُرِ ** (23) Thamud denied the warnings. (They denied) the warnings (الإنذارات) and admonitions (المواعظ), or the messengers (الرّسل). (٢٤) فَقالُوا أَبَشَراً مِنَّا ** (24) They said, 'Shall we follow a human being from among ourselves, from our own kind (جنسنا) واحِداً ** one single man? solitary (منفرداً), having no followers (لا تبع له) نَتَّبِعُهُ إِنَّا إِذاً لَفِي ضَلالٍ وَسُعُرٍ ** Indeed, in that case we shall be in manifest error and madness.' It is the plural of سعير (blazing fire), as if they reversed it against him (the Prophet), so they attributed to their following him what He (Allah) attributed to their abandoning their following him.

(٢٥) أَأُلْقِيَ الذِّكْرُ ** (Has the Reminder been cast upon him?) meaning the Book (الكتاب) and the Revelation (الوحي), عَلَيْهِ مِنْ بَيْنِنا ** (upon him from among us?) while among us are those who are more deserving of that than him. بَلْ هُوَ كَذَّابٌ أَشِرٌ ** (Rather he is a blatant liar) meaning his insolence (بطره) led him to exalt himself above us through his claim. (25) 'Has the Reminder been cast upon him from among us? Rather he is a blatant liar.'

(٢٦) سَيَعْلَمُونَ غَداً مَنِ الْكَذَّابُ الْأَشِرُ ** (Tomorrow they will know who the blatant liar is) the one whose insolence (اشره) led him to arrogance against the truth and seeking falsehood: is it Salih, or those who denied him? It was also recited as ستعلمون (you will know), based on a shift in address (الالتفات) or as a narration of what Salih answered them with. (26) Tomorrow they will know who the blatant liar is.

(٢٧) إِنَّا مُرْسِلُوا النَّاقَةِ ** (Indeed We are sending the she-camel) meaning, bringing her forth and sending her, فِتْنَةً لَهُمْ ** (as a trial for them) meaning, a test (اختباراً). فَارْتَقِبْهُمْ ** (So watch them) meaning, wait for them and observe what they do, وَاصْطَبِرْ ** (and be patient) regarding their harm (إذاهم). (27) Indeed We are sending the she-camel as a trial for them. So watch them and be patient.

(٢٨) وَنَبِّئْهُمْ أَنَّ الْماءَ قِسْمَةٌ بَيْنَهُمْ ** (And inform them that the water is to be shared between them) meaning, divided: a day for her and a day for them. كُلُّ شِرْبٍ مُحْتَضَرٌ ** (each turn of drinking being attended) meaning, its owner attends it during his turn (نوبته). (28) And inform them that the water is to be shared between them, each turn of drinking being attended.

(٢٩) فَنادَوْا صاحِبَهُمْ ** (Then they called their companion) Quddar ibn Salif ibn Uhaimar of Thamud. فَتَعاطى فَعَقَرَ ** (and he undertook [the task] and hamstrung [her]) meaning, he dared to undertake the killing of her, so he killed her; or, he undertook the sword (السّيف) and killed her. التعاطي (undertaking) is the taking of something with effort (بتكلّف). (29) Then they called their companion, and he undertook [the task] and hamstrung [her].

(٣٠) فَكَيْفَ كانَ عَذابِي وَنُذُرِ. (30) So how were My punishment and My warnings?

(٣١) إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ صَيْحَةً واحِدَةً فَكانُوا كَهَشِيمِ الْمُحْتَظِرِ ** (Indeed We sent against them a single Cry, and they became like the dry sticks of the pen-builder) like the dry grass (الحشيش) that the owner of the pen (الحظيرة) gathers for his livestock in winter. Their story has already passed in detail in Surat al-A'raf. (31) Indeed We sent against them a single Cry, and they became like the dry sticks of the pen-builder.

(٣٢) وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا الْقُرْآنَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ. (32) Certainly We have made the Quran easy for remembrance; so is there anyone who will remember?

(٣٣) كَذَّبَتْ قَوْمُ لُوطٍ بِالنُّذُرِ. (33) The people of Lot denied the warnings.

(٣٤) إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ حاصِباً ** (Indeed We sent against them a stone-shower) meaning, a wind (ريحاً) that pelted them with stones (بالحجارة), i.e., threw them at them, إِلَّا آلَ لُوطٍ نَجَّيْناهُمْ بِسَحَرٍ (except the family of Lot, whom We delivered at dawn) meaning, at the end of the night (في آخر اللّيل). (34) Indeed We sent against them a stone-shower, except the family of Lot, whom We delivered at dawn,

(٣٥) نِعْمَةً مِنْ عِنْدِنا ** (as a grace from Us) meaning, a bestowal of favor (انعاماً) from Us. كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي مَنْ شَكَرَ ** (Thus do We reward those who are grateful) meaning, those who were grateful for Our favor (نعمتنا) through faith (بالايمان) and obedience (والطاعة). (35) as a grace from Us. Thus do We reward those who are grateful.

(٣٦) وَلَقَدْ أَنْذَرَهُمْ ** (Certainly he had warned them) meaning, Lot, بَطْشَتَنا ** (of Our assault) meaning, Our seizing them with the punishment (بالعذاب). فَتَمارَوْا بِالنُّذُرِ ** (but they disputed the warnings) meaning, they denied the warnings (بالنّذر) contentiously (متشاكسين) and rejected the warning (بالإنذار) by way of false argumentation (الجدال بالباطل). (36) Certainly he had warned them of Our assault, but they disputed the warnings.

(٣٧) وَلَقَدْ راوَدُوهُ عَنْ ضَيْفِهِ ** (Certainly they solicited him concerning his guests) meaning, they intended immorality (الفجور) with them. فَطَمَسْنا أَعْيُنَهُمْ ** (so We blotted out their eyes) meaning, We wiped them out and leveled them with the rest of the face. Gabriel (جبرئيل) pointed his finger toward them, and their eyes vanished. In another narration (رواية): He took a handful of pebbles (بطحاء) and struck their faces with them, saying: "May the faces be disfigured (شاهت الوجوه)," so all the people of the city (المدينة) became blind. Both narrations (الروايتان) have preceded along with the complete story in Surat Hud. فَذُوقُوا عَذابِي وَنُذُرِ (Taste My punishment and My warnings!) So We said to them, "Taste," through the tongues of the angels (على السنة الملائكة) or by the apparent state of affairs (ظاهر الحال). (37) Certainly they solicited him concerning his guests, so We blotted out their eyes: 'Taste My punishment and My warnings!'

(٣٨) وَلَقَدْ صَبَّحَهُمْ بُكْرَةً عَذابٌ مُسْتَقِرٌّ ** (38) Certainly a lasting punishment overtook them early in the morning. It settles upon them until it delivers them to the Fire. (٣٩) فَذُوقُوا عَذابِي وَنُذُرِ. (39) 'So taste My punishment and My warnings!' (٤٠) وَلَقَدْ يَسَّرْنَا الْقُرْآنَ لِلذِّكْرِ فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ ** (40) Certainly We have made the Quran easy for admonishment; so is there anyone who will be admonished? He repeated that in every story as an indication that the denial of every messenger necessitates the descent of the punishment, and the listening to every story calls for remembrance (الادّكار) and taking heed (الاتعاظ), and as a renewal of warning (التنبيه) and awakening (الإيقاظ), lest heedlessness (السّهو) and negligence (الغفلة) overcome them. And similarly, the repetition of His saying: فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ (So which of your Lord’s bounties do you deny?) and *وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (Woe to the deniers on that day!) and similar phrases.

(٤١) وَلَقَدْ جاءَ آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ النُّذُرُ ** (41) Certainly the warnings came to Pharaoh’s clan. He sufficed Himself by mentioning them instead of mentioning him, due to the knowledge that he was more deserving of that. (٤٢) كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا كُلِّها ** (42) They denied all Our signs. It is said that this means the nine signs (الآيات التسع). And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means all of the successors (الأوصياء) (peace be upon them). **فَأَخَذْناهُمْ أَخْذَ عَزِيزٍ مُقْتَدِرٍ ** So We seized them with the seizing of One All-mighty, All-powerful. The seizing of One Who is not overcome and Whom nothing can frustrate.

(٤٣) أَكُفَّارُكُمْ خَيْرٌ مِنْ أُولئِكُمْ أَمْ لَكُمْ بَراءَةٌ فِي الزُّبُرِ ** (Are your disbelievers better than those, or do you have an immunity in the scriptures?) ** أَكُفَّارُكُمْ ** O assembly of Quraysh, ** خَيْرٌ مِنْ أُولئِكُمْ ** than these perished nations ** أَمْ لَكُمْ بَراءَةٌ فِي الزُّبُرِ ** meaning: Do you have an immunity in the scriptures (الكتب) that you will not perish as they perished? (٤٤) أَمْ يَقُولُونَ نَحْنُ جَمِيعٌ مُنْتَصِرٌ ** (Or do they say, 'We are a united host, and we shall be victorious'?) We are a united group (جماعة), our affair is gathered, victorious over the enemies; we shall not be defeated. Al-Qummi said: Quraysh said, "We have gathered to be victorious by killing you, O Muhammad." So Allah revealed ** أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ** the Ayah. (٤٥) سَيُهْزَمُ الْجَمْعُ وَيُوَلُّونَ الدُّبُرَ ** (Soon the host will be routed and turn their backs.) He said: This means the Day of Badr, when they were routed, captured, and killed. (٤٦) بَلِ السَّاعَةُ مَوْعِدُهُمْ وَالسَّاعَةُ أَدْهى وَأَمَرُّ ** (Rather the Hour is their tryst, and the Hour will be most calamitous and bitter.) ** بَلِ السَّاعَةُ مَوْعِدُهُمْ ** meaning: The Resurrection (القيامة) is the appointed time for their original punishment, and for what afflicts them in this world (الدنيا) from its harbingers ** وَالسَّاعَةُ أَدْهى وَأَمَرُّ ** more severe (أشدّ), harsher (أغلظ), and bitterer in taste (مذاقاً) than the punishment of this world (الدنيا). (٤٧) إِنَّ الْمُجْرِمِينَ فِي ضَلالٍ وَسُعُرٍ ** (Indeed, the guilty are in error and madness.) ** إِنَّ الْمُجْرِمِينَ فِي ضَلالٍ ** away from the truth (الحق) in this world (الدنيا) ** وَسُعُرٍ ** and fires (نيران) in the Hereafter (الآخرة). Al-Qummi said: And Sa'ir (سعير) is a great valley (واد) in Hell (جهنّم). (٤٨) يَوْمَ يُسْحَبُونَ فِي النَّارِ عَلى وُجُوهِهِمْ ذُوقُوا مَسَّ سَقَرَ ** (The day they are dragged into the Fire on their faces: 'Taste the touch of Saqar!') ** يَوْمَ يُسْحَبُونَ فِي النَّارِ عَلى وُجُوهِهِمْ ** They are dragged upon it ** ذُوقُوا مَسَّ سَقَرَ ** It will be said to them: Taste the heat (حرّ) of the Fire (النّار) and its pain (ألمها). It was said: Saqar (سقر) is a proper name (علم) for Hell (جهنّم). In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, in Hell (جهنّم) there is a valley (وادياً) for the arrogant (المتكبّرين) called Saqar (سقر). It complained to Allah about the intensity of its heat and asked Him to permit it to breathe, and it burned Hell (جهنم).

(٤٩) إِنَّا كُلَّ شَيْءٍ خَلَقْناهُ بِقَدَرٍ ** (49) Indeed, We have created everything in measure. predetermined, written in the Tablet (اللوح) before its occurrence. Al-Qummi** said: It has a time, a term, and a duration.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: Indeed, the Qadariyyah (القدرية) are the Magians (مجوس) of this nation. They are those who desired to describe Allah with His justice, but thereby removed Him from His sovereignty. And concerning them, this verse was revealed: يَوْمَ يُسْحَبُونَ ** (The day they are dragged) up to His saying: بِقَدَرٍ (in measure). And He was asked about incantations (الرقى): "Do they repel anything from the decree (القدر)?" So He said: "They are part of the decree (القدر)."

And in Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: Allah did not reveal these verses except concerning the Qadariyyah (القدرية): إِنَّ الْمُجْرِمِينَ (Indeed, the guilty ones) up to His saying: بِقَدَرٍ. (in measure).

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This verse was revealed concerning the Qadariyyah (القدرية): ذُوقُوا مَسَّ سَقَرَ إِنَّا كُلَّ شَيْءٍ خَلَقْناهُ بِقَدَرٍ. (Taste the touch of hell! Indeed, We have created everything in measure.)

And Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: I found a name for the people of al-Qadar (أهل القدر) in the Book of Allah: إِنَّ الْمُجْرِمِينَ (Indeed, the guilty ones) up to His saying: بِقَدَرٍ ** (in measure). He said: So they are the guilty ones (المجرمون).

(٥٠) وَما أَمْرُنا إِلَّا واحِدَةٌ ** (50) And Our command is but a single [word] Al-Qummi (said): meaning [He] says, 'Be,' and it is, like the twinkling of an eye. (This refers to) the ease and speed. (٥١) وَلَقَدْ أَهْلَكْنا أَشْياعَكُمْ ** (51) Certainly We have destroyed your likes. (Meaning) your followers and those similar to you in disbelief, among the idol worshippers. فَهَلْ مِنْ مُدَّكِرٍ (Is there any who will be admonished?) (Meaning) one who takes heed (متّعظ). (٥٢) وَكُلُّ شَيْءٍ فَعَلُوهُ فِي الزُّبُرِ ** (52) And everything they have done is in the scriptures. (Meaning) written in the books of the recording angels (الحفظة). (٥٣) وَكُلُّ صَغِيرٍ وَكَبِيرٍ ** (53) And every small and great [deed] of the deeds مُسْتَطَرٌ ** (is inscribed.) (Meaning) written down (مسطور). (٥٤) إِنَّ الْمُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّاتٍ وَنَهَرٍ. (54) Indeed the Godwary will be amid gardens and rivers, (٥٥) فِي مَقْعَدِ صِدْقٍ ** (55) in a truthful station, in a pleasing place (مكان مرضيّ) or a true place (حقّ) where there is no idle talk (لغو) nor sinfulness (تأثيم) عِنْدَ مَلِيكٍ مُقْتَدِرٍ ** (with an Omnipotent Sovereign.) being brought near (مقرّبين) to the One Whose command is exalted in sovereignty (الملك) and power (الاقتدار), such that those possessing understanding (ذووا الافهام) are bewildered by Him.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surat Iqtarabat al-Sa'ah (The Hour has drawn near) [Surah 54], Allah will bring him forth from his grave upon a she-camel from the she-camels of Paradise, if Allah wills.

سُورَة الرّحْمن

Meccan, and it is said Medinan. The number of its verses is seventy-eight verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ. In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
